Frequently Asked Questions

Find answers to common questions about 3D printing in education and its professional applications.

3D printing is revolutionizing various industries, creating diverse career opportunities:

  • Product Design:Working on prototypes and consumer products across multiple industries.
  • Engineering:Mechanical, aerospace, biomedical, and civil engineering for creating critical parts and components.
  • Architecture:Developing highly detailed architectural models and complex structural designs.
  • Healthcare:Creating custom prosthetics, dental implants, and patient-specific surgical models.
  • Education:Teaching 3D printing techniques and managing digital fabrication labs in schools.
No, students do not need extensive prior technical knowledge to begin with 3D printing. Our introductory courses are structured to provide foundational knowledge, making it easy for beginners of all ages to engage with and master the technology through a step-by-step approach.
Yes, our training materials are specifically tailored for educational environments. They include comprehensive lesson plans, hands-on projects, and assessments that align with current educational standards, ensuring a seamless integration into the school curriculum.
Yes, we offer numerous after-school programs focusing specifically on 3D printing and STEM. These programs encourage students to explore technology and creativity in a less formal, high-energy setting that complements regular classroom hours.
3D printing is integral to STEM as it enhances critical thinking, creativity, and problem-solving skills. It allows students to apply theoretical concepts in a hands-on context, bridging the gap between abstract scientific principles and real-world engineering and mathematics.

We utilize industry-standard software adapted for different learning levels:

Tinkercad

Perfect for grades 3-8, offering a beginner-friendly introduction to 3D design.

Fusion 360

For grades 9-12, providing advanced capabilities used in professional manufacturing.

Astroprint

A cloud platform for seamless file transfer and efficient print management.

Advanced Tools

Blender and SketchUp are available depending on course objectives and student level.

3D printing fosters creativity by providing a medium where digital ideas become physical reality:

  • Prototype ideas and see them come to life instantly.
  • Iterate designs easily through experimentation.
  • Personalize projects to reflect unique perspectives.
  • Collaborate with peers on complex problem-solving.
Mech It Easy

Hands-on STEM kits, built for Nepal's classrooms. Empowering the next generation of engineers through hand-on experimentation.
